title: Goal Contracts and Observable Completion · Loop Forensics
summary: Turn a vague long-running objective into an executable goal contract. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a measurable goal and done-condition specification and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Write a goal as an outcome rather than an unbounded activity.
Attach observable success measures, boundaries, and evidence requirements.
Separate current objective, durable constraints, and optional next work.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Bounded agent-loop portfolio. No external effects occur in this browser lab."""

steps.title: Inspect the failure record

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Write a goal as an outcome rather than an unbounded activity. Artifact under review: a measurable goal and done-condition specification.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Goal Outcome, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: loops-goal-hierarchy-r3o1zv" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Goal Hierarchy into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: loops-goal-hierarchy-r3o1zv
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Goal Hierarchy applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Relate mission, milestone, task, and step without losing the terminal outcome.
